Do you agree? Do you think your morality affects your mental health? Why? / Why not? Body Mind Unit goals Learn how to make requests, offers, and suggestions. Talk about hypothetical future situations. Practise the / / sound. Show respect for different cultures and realities. 32 33 Presentation Checkpoint / Reflect Each unit begins with two pages that will encourage discussion in the class. These pages introduce the theme of the unit. It also shows the unit goals. In each unit, you will have Checkpoint and Reflect sections. These activities will foster your creativity, collaboration, and critical thinking skills. Reviews Think Back / 3Ws Extra Material Each unit presents instances where you can evaluate your learning of the structures and vocabulary of each lesson via activities and games. At the end of each lesson and unit, we invite you to reflect on your learning process by answering questions about the themes seen during the lesson and overall unit. Throughout the book, you will have Extra Material given by your teacher to help you complete certain activities. Project 1 2 ut Create a Mural Abo Social Issue a Discuss social issues 3 and discuss. If you need, you can Lesson E B Project 1 My style 57 p. 35 56 Unit 2. Body, Mind, Student’s Book pp. 8-9 Language in Use 1 Reading and Spirit 1 2 Look at the pictures. Where can these kinds of houses be found? Read the text. Guess which of the marked words are the correct ones. Hi, honey! Are you having fun with your The Beauty the verbs in brackets. Hi mum! Yes, it’s great. I have been (be) with Pascal for three days and we a. (already / walk) around the city a lot. Nature imposes its conditions on Chile. As a result, Chileans have adapted their houses to tolerate the demands of nature and the variety of climates. Fantastic! What b. In the north of Chile, many people live in low one-story homes made of a. stone / wood, mud, and straw. That is the case in San Pedro de Atacama, a highly touristic area located in the b. driest / largest desert in the world. Your Exercise Book contains extra material for you to reinforce what you have learnt in each lesson. It contains, at the end of each unit, a Test Yourself section, where you can measure your knowledge and an assessment sheet for the unit project. the correct present perfect form of friend in Paris? of Diversity Exercise Book Student’s Book p. 10 Present Perfect Read the text. Then, complete with (you / eat)? I never have. You’ll have to learn the recipe. Oh, I c. (just / have) the best cheese ever! And tonight we’ll have authentic ratatoui lle. d. (you / eat) it? You e. In the centre of Chile, we find Valparaíso, c. which / who was declared a Unesco World Heritage Site, due to the ingenuity of its architectural solutions. Homes in Valparaíso are d. uncoloured / colourful, made of various materials, and many are decorated with street art. They are located on the e. flatland / hillside, giving the city its characteristic landscape. (not / lose) hope yet, right? Lol. Of course not! Honey, sorry, all this talk got me hungry. Keep having fun. I hope you f. (not / forget) about getting me a souvenir! I g. I love you too! (already / buy) it. Talk to you later. I love you mum! I have just begun saving to renovate my room. In the south of Chile, on the f. peninsula / island of Chiloe, peculiar houses are placed on stilts buried in the beach. The sturdy g. wooden / cement poles supporting the traditional stilted houses, or palafitos, are built to protect houses from the rising and falling h. tides / rocks, and they’ve made the island famous around the world. Together, these creative designs form the architecture of a country with not only diverse geography and constructions, but diverse people as well. Let’s invite the rest of the world to visit and appreciate Chile’s i. cultural / natural contribution. 2 3 4 3 Listen and circle the correct answers. Circle the mistakes. Correct them. Were your guesses from Activity 1 correct? e.g. Chilean homes represent the monotony of the landscape. a. Chileans don’t adapt their homes to the different climates. b. Homes in Valparaíso are dark and undecorated. c. Palafitos protect houses from the sun and wind. 4 Order these sentences. Add just, yet, or already. e.g. saving / have / begun / I / to renovate my room a. a new duvet / has / My granny / gotten Adapted from This is Chile: Architecture b. chosen / have / the paint colour the diversity of the landscape. c. has / money for new curtains / given 3 me / I / not me / My aunt Talk about things you’ve recently done. Use just, yet, and already. Unit 1. My Home Lesson 1. My style 5 Icons E B p. 51 This icon indicates an activity in pairs or groups. Review) • Listening • CLIL / Arts • Speaking 72 Language in Use (Past Simple and Past Continuous) • Listening • CLIL / Technology • Speaking 89 Pronunciation (Stressed syllables) 91 • Reading 74 75 Pronunciation (Initial /st/ sound) 75 • Reading • Writing (A Biography) • Review 76 • Final Review • Project 80 78 79 82 90 91 92 Language in Use (Past Perfect) 93 • Writing (A News Piece) • Review 94 Lesson 2 What Your Future Holds 96 • Reading 73 88 95 96 Language in Use (Present Perfect Continuous) 98 • Listening • CLIL / Language and Communication • Speaking • Reading • Writing (A Story) • Review 99 100 101 102 104 105 • Final Review • Project 106 • Glossary • Extra Reading 1 • Extra Reading 2 • Extra Reading 3 • Extra Reading 4 • Extra Reading 5 • Irregular Verbs • Bibliography 110 108 114 116 118 120 122 124 125 Contents CL0000000001368 INICIALES_SB_Ing_2M_Lic2020_7469.indd 5 5 04-08-22 17:40 Unit 1 Our Home Look and discuss What current affair does this picture represent? How does this global problem affect you and your home? What daily habits can you change to help the planet? Unit goals Express opinions – agreeing and disagreeing. Describe actions that occurred in the past with present consequences. Describe quantity and frequency. Practice initial /t/ and /d/ sounds. Show a positive attitude about your learning progress. 6 CL0000000001368 Ing_2M_U1_Lesson 1_Lic2020_7061.indd 6 04-08-22 17:43 7 CL0000000001368 Ing_2M_U1_Lesson 1_Lic2020_7061.indd 7 04-08-22 17:43 on 1 My Style L ess Blog Reading 1 Think of words you know for bedroom furniture. Make a spidergram in your notebook with the words you remember. ? Bed This is my new bedroom. My family and I have just moved to a new house. I’ve never had my own space before – I used to share a room with my brother. As you can see, I'm pretty tidy. You can also tell I love science... Look at those planets over my bed! Diego Bedroom furniture ? Teenage Bedrooms ? 2 Read a blog about teenage bedrooms. Write any new furniture words in your spidergram from Activity 1. 3 Complete the sentences in your notebook with the correct names. e.g. Silvia has a small bed. a. … is learning to play guitar. b. … plays computer games. c. … has just changed rooms. d. … shares a room. e. … hasn't decorated his/her room much. This is a typical day in my room. Dad went to work, and my little sister is still sleeping late. My bed here is small, but at least I sleep alone. Don't get me wrong! I love sharing the space with them, but they can get really messy sometimes! Good thing this girl here always helps me put everything in order every day. Silvia f. … hasn't had his/her own room before. 4 Discuss your ideal bedroom. Think about: colour - decoration - furniture 8 Unit 1. Our Home CL0000000001368 Ing_2M_U1_Lesson 1_Lic2020_7061.indd 8 04-08-22 17:43 Have you just decorated your room? Or do you simply love your space and want to share it with us? Post a picture and description on this site! 5 OMG, my room is so messy already! I just cleaned it yesterday. My favourite place is my desk. I spend all my free time sitting there playing computer games. I made sure to get a comfortable chair. I always keep my blinds closed because I don’t like watching people go by. In your notebook, write a description of your own bedroom. Use vocabulary from the text and the words below to help you. bedside table - duvet - chest of drawers bedside lamp - TV - wardrobe - computer Checkpoint Laura 6 Present your bedroom to the class. a. Make a sketch of your room. b. Use the description you wrote in Activity 5. c. Present your room to the class. m My Bedroo Here’s a pic of my room! I haven’t really decorated it much. The white walls make the room feel bigger, and the big window gives me lots of light. I’m really excited about my new guitar – I just got it a week ago! I’m still learning how to play it, though. to the class. m My Bedroo Here’s a pic of my room! I haven’t really decorated it much. The white walls make the room feel bigger, and the big window gives me lots of light. I’m really excited about my new guitar – I just got it a week ago! I’m still learning how to play it, though. Samuel My bedroom has a bed, a desk, and a chair.
